task_completion.description: Open and read the specification brief on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or document viewer is open showing the contents of 'rainwater_system_spec.txt' from the Desktop.

task_completion.description: Open the FZK-Haus IFC model in Bonsai/BlenderBIM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Blender is open with the Bonsai/BlenderBIM interface active, and the FZK-Haus architectural model is visible in the 3D viewport or Outliner.

task_completion.description: Model a rainwater storage tank (IfcTank) and a distribution pump (IfcPump).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Outliner or Object Properties panel shows newly created objects classified as 'IfcTank' and 'IfcPump'.

task_completion.description: Model at least two connecting pipe segments (IfcPipeSegment).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Blender Outliner or 3D viewport shows at least two distinct objects classified as 'IfcPipeSegment' positioned to connect the system.

task_completion.description: Assign the new elements to a new IfcSystem named 'Rainwater'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bonsai Systems/Groups panel or Outliner shows an IfcSystem explicitly named 'Rainwater', and the tank, pump, and pipe segments are shown as assigned to this system.

task_completion.description: Save the enriched project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, Blender header, or file explorer shows the file being saved as 'fzk_rainwater_system.ifc' in the '/home/ga/BIMProjects/' directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the Blender/Bonsai graphical interface to model the system.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Blender menus, Bonsai toolbars, and the 3D viewport, rather than just executing a terminal script to generate the IFC file.

integrity.description: The original FZK-Haus architectural elements were preserved.
integrity.visual_evidence: The original building elements (walls, roof, windows) remain visible in the 3D viewport and Outliner alongside the new plumbing elements; the agent did not start from a blank file.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely interacted with the software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a logical progression of modeling steps (selecting tools, placing objects, assigning properties) rather than instantly jumping to a completed state.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must read the spec document at '/home/ga/Desktop/rainwater_system_spec.txt'. The final model must be saved to '/home/ga/BIMProjects/fzk_rainwater_system.ifc'. The model must contain an 'IfcTank', an 'IfcPump', and at least two 'IfcPipeSegment' objects. These elements must be assigned to an IfcSystem named 'Rainwater' (or containing keywords like 'rainwater', 'harvest', 'reclaimed', 'non-potable'). Original architectural elements of the FZK-Haus model must be preserved.
